 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Unit tests for the Codex round-2 hardening of the analysis-snapshot
     2	# RELEASE Zenodo upload/DOI-record-back logic (#573 Slice C):
     3	# api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-upload.R.
     4	#
     5	# Split out of the sibling test-unit-analysis-release-zenodo-upload.R (which
     6	# covers the pre-existing upload/deposition-lifecycle behavior) to keep both
     7	# files under the repo's 600-line soft ceiling -- mirrors the
     8	# `-docs.R`/`-verify.R` and `comparisons-functions.R`/`comparisons-parsers.R`
     9	# split precedent.
    10	#
    11	# Pure, DB-free, NO NETWORK: every HTTP boundary is an injectable seam
    12	# (`patch`/`record_doi_fn`) so tests supply plain stub closures instead of a
    13	# mocking library or a real network call.
    14	#
    15	# Covers:
    16	# - item 2 (HIGH): `record_doi()`, `manual_doi_command()`, and
    17	#   `print_doi_record_back()` all reject an invalid `--release-id` (a quote,
    18	#   `;`, embedded newline, or `../` traversal shape) BEFORE building any
    19	#   admin PATCH URL or printed shell command from it.
    20	# - item 2 (HIGH) defense in depth: `manual_doi_command()` `shQuote()`s the
    21	#   URL and JSON body it prints.
    22	# - item 3 (MEDIUM): `print_doi_record_back()` never prints a populated PATCH
    23	#   command for a DRAFT upload (published-only rule), regardless of whether
    24	#   `--record-doi` was passed.
    25
    26	library(testthat)
    27
    28	source_api_file("functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-upload.R", local = FALSE)
    29
    30	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    31	# record_doi / manual_doi_command -- release-id validation (Codex round-2
    32	# item 2, HIGH): an invalid --release-id must be rejected BEFORE either
    33	# builds the admin PATCH URL or the printed shell command from it.
    34	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    35
    36	.invalid_release_ids_round2 <- list(
    37	  quote = "asr_deadbeefcafeb' OR '1'='1",
    38	  semicolon = "asr_deadbeefcafeb; rm -rf ~",
    39	  newline = "asr_deadbeefcafeb\nrm -rf /",
    40	  traversal = "../evil"
    41	)
    42
    43	for (.label in names(.invalid_release_ids_round2)) {
    44	  local({
    45	    label <- .label
    46	    bad_id <- .invalid_release_ids_round2[[label]]
    47
    48	    test_that(paste0("record_doi: rejects a release_id containing ", label, " before calling patch"), {
    49	      called <- FALSE
    50	      stub_patch <- function(method, url, token, body = NULL) {
    51	        called <<- TRUE
    52	        list()
    53	      }
    54	      expect_error(
    55	        analysis_release_zenodo_record_doi(
    56	          "http://localhost:7778", "admin-token", bad_id,
    57	          doi_fields = list(zenodo_record_id = "1"), patch = stub_patch
    58	        ),
    59	        "Invalid analysis-snapshot release id"
    60	      )
    61	      expect_false(called)
    62	    })
    63
    64	    test_that(paste0("manual_doi_command: rejects a release_id containing ", label, " before building any command"), {
    65	      expect_error(
    66	        analysis_release_zenodo_manual_doi_command(
    67	          "http://localhost:7778", bad_id, doi_fields = list(zenodo_record_id = "1")
    68	        ),
    69	        "Invalid analysis-snapshot release id"
    70	      )
    71	    })
    72	  })
    73	}
    74
    75	test_that("record_doi: accepts a well-formed asr_<16 hex> release_id", {
    76	  captured_url <- NULL
    77	  stub_patch <- function(method, url, token, body = NULL) {
    78	    captured_url <<- url
    79	    list()
    80	  }
    81	  analysis_release_zenodo_record_doi(
    82	    "http://localhost:7778", "admin-token", "asr_deadbeefcafebabe",
    83	    doi_fields = list(zenodo_record_id = "1"), patch = stub_patch
    84	  )
    85	  expect_identical(captured_url, "http://localhost:7778/api/admin/analysis/releases/asr_deadbeefcafebabe/doi")
    86	})
    87
    88	test_that("manual_doi_command: shQuote()s the URL and JSON body (defense in depth, Codex round-2 item 2)", {
    89	  # A well-formed release_id can never itself carry shell metacharacters (the
    90	  # validator rejects those), but a doi_fields VALUE is not release-id
    91	  # shaped -- shQuote() on the whole JSON body means even an adversarial
    92	  # field value cannot break out of the printed `curl` command's arguments.
    93	  fields <- list(zenodo_record_id = "1' ; rm -rf ~ ; #")
    94	  command <- analysis_release_zenodo_manual_doi_command(
    95	    "http://localhost:7778", "asr_deadbeefcafebabe", doi_fields = fields
    96	  )
    97
    98	  expected_url <- "http://localhost:7778/api/admin/analysis/releases/asr_deadbeefcafebabe/doi"
    99	  expected_body_json <- as.character(jsonlite::toJSON(fields, auto_unbox = TRUE))
   100
   101	  expect_true(grepl(shQuote(expected_url), command, fixed = TRUE))
   102	  expect_true(grepl(shQuote(expected_body_json), command, fixed = TRUE))
   103	  # The dangerous value must never appear as a NAIVELY single-quoted,
   104	  # unescaped argument -- that would let a POSIX shell split it into
   105	  # multiple commands at the un-escaped `;`.
   106	  expect_false(grepl("-d '1' ; rm -rf ~ ; #'", command, fixed = TRUE))
   107	})
   108
   109	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   110	# print_doi_record_back -- the CLI-facing DOI print step. Codex round-2
   111	# item 2 (HIGH, release-id validation before ANY URL/command is built) and
   112	# item 3 (MEDIUM, no populated PATCH command for a DRAFT).
   113	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   114
   115	.captured_prints <- function() {
   116	  out <- character(0)
   117	  list(
   118	    printer = function(...) out <<- c(out, paste0(..., collapse = "")),
   119	    get = function() paste(out, collapse = "")
   120	  )
   121	}
   122
   123	.published_result <- list(
   124	  deposition_id = 555,
   125	  reserved_doi = "10.5281/zenodo.555",
   126	  draft_url = "https://zenodo.org/deposit/555",
   127	  published = TRUE,
   128	  version_doi = "10.5281/zenodo.555",
   129	  concept_doi = "10.5281/zenodo.554",
   130	  record_url = "https://zenodo.org/record/555"
   131	)
   132
   133	.draft_result <- list(
   134	  deposition_id = 555,
   135	  reserved_doi = "10.5281/zenodo.555",
   136	  draft_url = "https://zenodo.org/deposit/555",
   137	  published = FALSE,
   138	  version_doi = NA_character_,
   139	  concept_doi = NA_character_,
   140	  record_url = NA_character_
   141	)
   142
   143	test_that("print_doi_record_back: DRAFT upload prints NO populated PATCH command (Codex round-2 item 3, MEDIUM)", {
   144	  captured <- .captured_prints()
   145	  analysis_release_zenodo_print_doi_record_back(
   146	    .draft_result, "asr_deadbeefcafebabe", "http://localhost:7778",
   147	    record_doi = FALSE, printer = captured$printer
   148	  )
   149	  output <- captured$get()
   150
   151	  expect_false(grepl("curl -X PATCH", output, fixed = TRUE))
   152	  expect_false(grepl("/doi", output, fixed = TRUE))
   153	  expect_true(grepl("not published", output, fixed = TRUE) || grepl("Draft", output, fixed = TRUE))
   154	  expect_true(grepl("--publish --confirm-publish", output, fixed = TRUE))
   155	})
   156
   157	test_that("print_doi_record_back: DRAFT upload with --record-doi still prints NO PATCH command (published-only rule)", {
   158	  # Even if the operator passed --record-doi, a draft's DOI is never final --
   159	  # the published-only rule must win regardless of the record_doi flag.
   160	  captured <- .captured_prints()
   161	  withr::local_envvar(SYSNDD_ADMIN_TOKEN = "admin-token")
   162	  analysis_release_zenodo_print_doi_record_back(
   163	    .draft_result, "asr_deadbeefcafebabe", "http://localhost:7778",
   164	    record_doi = TRUE, printer = captured$printer
   165	  )
   166	  output <- captured$get()
   167
   168	  expect_false(grepl("curl -X PATCH", output, fixed = TRUE))
   169	  expect_false(grepl("DOI recorded on the SysNDD release head", output, fixed = TRUE))
   170	})
   171
   172	test_that("print_doi_record_back: PUBLISHED upload without --record-doi prints the populated manual command", {
   173	  captured <- .captured_prints()
   174	  analysis_release_zenodo_print_doi_record_back(
   175	    .published_result, "asr_deadbeefcafebabe", "http://localhost:7778",
   176	    record_doi = FALSE, printer = captured$printer
   177	  )
   178	  output <- captured$get()
   179
   180	  expect_true(grepl("curl -X PATCH", output, fixed = TRUE))
   181	  expect_true(grepl("10.5281/zenodo.555", output, fixed = TRUE))
   182	})
   183
   184	test_that("print_doi_record_back: PUBLISHED upload with --record-doi and admin token auto-records via the injected seam (no manual command printed)", {
   185	  captured <- .captured_prints()
   186	  withr::local_envvar(SYSNDD_ADMIN_TOKEN = "admin-token")
   187	  captured_call <- new.env()
   188	  stub_record_doi_fn <- function(sysndd_api_base_url, admin_token, release_id, doi_fields) {
   189	    assign("release_id", release_id, envir = captured_call)
   190	    assign("doi_fields", doi_fields, envir = captured_call)
   191	    list(release_id = release_id, version_doi = doi_fields$version_doi, zenodo_record_url = doi_fields$zenodo_record_url)
   192	  }
   193
   194	  analysis_release_zenodo_print_doi_record_back(
   195	    .published_result, "asr_deadbeefcafebabe", "http://localhost:7778",
   196	    record_doi = TRUE, printer = captured$printer, record_doi_fn = stub_record_doi_fn
   197	  )
   198	  output <- captured$get()
   199
   200	  expect_false(grepl("curl -X PATCH", output, fixed = TRUE))
   201	  expect_true(grepl("DOI recorded on the SysNDD release head", output, fixed = TRUE))
   202	  expect_identical(get("release_id", envir = captured_call), "asr_deadbeefcafebabe")
   203	  expect_identical(get("doi_fields", envir = captured_call)$version_doi, "10.5281/zenodo.555")
   204	})
   205
   206	for (.label in names(.invalid_release_ids_round2)) {
   207	  local({
   208	    label <- .label
   209	    bad_id <- .invalid_release_ids_round2[[label]]
   210
   211	    test_that(paste0(
   212	      "print_doi_record_back: rejects a release_id containing ", label,
   213	      " before building any URL/command (Codex round-2 item 2, HIGH)"
   214	    ), {
   215	      captured <- .captured_prints()
   216	      expect_error(
   217	        analysis_release_zenodo_print_doi_record_back(
   218	          .published_result, bad_id, "http://localhost:7778",
   219	          record_doi = FALSE, printer = captured$printer
   220	        ),
   221	        "Invalid analysis-snapshot release id"
   222	      )
   223	      # Nothing (no manual command, no partial URL) was ever printed before
   224	      # the validator stopped the run.
   225	      expect_false(grepl("curl", captured$get(), fixed = TRUE))
   226	    })
   227	  })
   228	}
   229
   230	test_that("print_doi_record_back: no --release-id supplied prints guidance and never validates/builds anything", {
   231	  captured <- .captured_prints()
   232	  expect_no_error(
   233	    analysis_release_zenodo_print_doi_record_back(
   234	      .published_result, NULL, "http://localhost:7778",
   235	      record_doi = FALSE, printer = captured$printer
   236	    )
   237	  )
   238	  expect_true(grepl("No --release-id supplied", captured$get(), fixed = TRUE))
   239	})
     1	# Unit tests for the analysis-snapshot RELEASE Zenodo fetch/extract-verify
     2	# helpers and safety validator (#573 Slice C, Codex round-1 hardening):
     3	# api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-verify.R.
     4	#
     5	# Pure, DB-free, NO NETWORK: every HTTP boundary is an injectable seam
     6	# (`http_get_json`/`http_download`) so tests supply plain stub closures
     7	# instead of a mocking library. This file sources the MAIN
     8	# `analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-package.R` (not `-verify.R` directly)
     9	# because that file defines `%||%` and guard-sources BOTH `-docs.R` and
    10	# `-verify.R` into the same target environment -- mirrors
    11	# `test-unit-analysis-release-zenodo-package.R`'s sourcing.
    12	#
    13	# Covers: release-id shape validation (path/filename/marker-injection guard),
    14	# `fetch_head`/`download_bundle` URL-building, `extract_and_verify`
    15	# (checksum coverage + path-traversal rejection), and the safety validator
    16	# `validate_staging` (case-insensitive forbidden-name matching, the file-type
    17	# allowlist, and symlink rejection).
    18
    19	library(testthat)
    20
    21	source_api_file("functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-package.R", local = FALSE)
    22
    23	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    24	# Shared fixture helpers
    25	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    26
    27	# A well-formed Zenodo staging directory: the expected top-level docs plus the
    28	# nested analysis_snapshot_release/ subdir with its own manifest+checksums.
    29	#
    30	# Deliberately uses plain `tempfile()`, NOT `withr::local_tempdir()`: the
    31	# latter schedules cleanup via `withr::defer(..., envir = parent.frame())`,
    32	# and `parent.frame()` evaluated INSIDE this helper resolves to this
    33	# function's own (short-lived) call frame, not the calling `test_that()`
    34	# block -- so the directory would be deleted the instant this helper
    35	# returns, before the caller can use it. Session tempdir cleanup at process
    36	# exit is sufficient for a short-lived test run.
    37	make_well_formed_staging <- function() {
    38	  staging <- file.path(tempdir(), paste0("zenodo_staging_", as.integer(stats::runif(1, 1, 1e9))))
    39	  dir.create(staging, recursive = TRUE)
    40	  for (name in c(
    41	    "README.md", "DATA_CARD.md", "SCHEMA.md", "CHANGELOG.md", "CITATION.cff",
    42	    "datapackage.json", "zenodo_metadata.json", "checksums.sha256"
    43	  )) {
    44	    writeLines("placeholder content", file.path(staging, name))
    45	  }
    46	  dir.create(file.path(staging, "analysis_snapshot_release"))
    47	  writeLines('{"release_id":"asr_test"}', file.path(staging, "analysis_snapshot_release", "manifest.json"))
    48	  writeLines("deadbeef  manifest.json", file.path(staging, "analysis_snapshot_release", "checksums.sha256"))
    49	  staging
    50	}
    51
    52	# Builds a tiny tar.gz with two files + a matching (or deliberately wrong,
    53	# omitted, duplicated, or traversal-shaped) inner checksums.sha256, mirroring
    54	# the shape of a real release bundle (files directly at the archive root --
    55	# NOT nested under a named subdirectory, unlike the sibling nddscore
    56	# archive).
    57	#
    58	# @param omit_checksum_for character vector of "a.txt"/"b.txt" to leave OUT
    59	#   of checksums.sha256 while still including the file in the tar (proves
    60	#   the coverage gap check, item 4).
    61	# @param extra_checksum_lines extra raw lines appended verbatim to
    62	#   checksums.sha256 (proves the path-traversal + duplicate-entry checks,
    63	#   item 4).
    64	build_fixture_bundle <- function(
    65	    dir, a_sha_override = NULL, b_sha_override = NULL,
    66	    omit_checksum_for = character(0), extra_checksum_lines = character(0)) {
    67	  src <- file.path(dir, paste0("src_", as.integer(stats::runif(1, 1, 1e9))))
    68	  dir.create(src, recursive = TRUE)
    69	  writeLines("file A content", file.path(src, "a.txt"))
    70	  writeLines("file B content", file.path(src, "b.txt"))
    71	  a_sha <- a_sha_override %||% digest::digest(file = file.path(src, "a.txt"), algo = "sha256")
    72	  b_sha <- b_sha_override %||% digest::digest(file = file.path(src, "b.txt"), algo = "sha256")
    73
    74	  lines <- character(0)
    75	  if (!("a.txt" %in% omit_checksum_for)) lines <- c(lines, paste0(a_sha, "  a.txt"))
    76	  if (!("b.txt" %in% omit_checksum_for)) lines <- c(lines, paste0(b_sha, "  b.txt"))
    77	  lines <- c(lines, extra_checksum_lines)
    78	  cat(paste0(paste(lines, collapse = "\n"), "\n"), file = file.path(src, "checksums.sha256"))
    79
    80	  bundle_path <- file.path(dir, paste0("bundle_", basename(src), ".tar.gz"))
    81	  withr::with_dir(src, {
    82	    utils::tar(
    83	      tarfile = bundle_path, files = c("a.txt", "b.txt", "checksums.sha256"),
    84	      compression = "gzip", tar = "internal"
    85	    )
    86	  })
    87	  bundle_path
    88	}
    89
    90	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    91	# Release-id shape validation (item 2) -- exercised via fetch_head(), the
    92	# public entry point for the REQUEST-arg validation.
    93	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    94
    95	test_that("fetch_head: rejects a release_id containing '..' (path traversal shape) before calling http_get_json", {
    96	  called <- FALSE
    97	  stub_get_json <- function(url) {
    98	    called <<- TRUE
    99	    list()
   100	  }
   101	  expect_error(
   102	    analysis_release_zenodo_fetch_head("http://localhost:7778", "../evil", http_get_json = stub_get_json),
   103	    "Invalid analysis-snapshot release id"
   104	  )
   105	  expect_false(called)
   106	})
   107
   108	test_that("fetch_head: rejects a release_id containing an embedded newline before calling http_get_json", {
   109	  called <- FALSE
   110	  stub_get_json <- function(url) {
   111	    called <<- TRUE
   112	    list()
   113	  }
   114	  expect_error(
   115	    analysis_release_zenodo_fetch_head(
   116	      "http://localhost:7778", "asr_1234567890abcd\nrm -rf /", http_get_json = stub_get_json
   117	    ),
   118	    "Invalid analysis-snapshot release id"
   119	  )
   120	  expect_false(called)
   121	})
   122
   123	test_that("fetch_head: rejects a release_id containing shell metacharacters before calling http_get_json", {
   124	  called <- FALSE
   125	  stub_get_json <- function(url) {
   126	    called <<- TRUE
   127	    list()
   128	  }
   129	  expect_error(
   130	    analysis_release_zenodo_fetch_head(
   131	      "http://localhost:7778", "asr_1234567890abcd;rm -rf /", http_get_json = stub_get_json
   132	    ),
   133	    "Invalid analysis-snapshot release id"
   134	  )
   135	  expect_false(called)
   136	})
   137
   138	test_that("fetch_head: rejects an uppercase / wrong-length release_id", {
   139	  stub_get_json <- function(url) list()
   140	  expect_error(
   141	    analysis_release_zenodo_fetch_head("http://localhost:7778", "ASR_DEADBEEFCAFEBABE", http_get_json = stub_get_json),
   142	    "Invalid analysis-snapshot release id"
   143	  )
   144	  expect_error(
   145	    analysis_release_zenodo_fetch_head("http://localhost:7778", "asr_short", http_get_json = stub_get_json),
   146	    "Invalid analysis-snapshot release id"
   147	  )
   148	})
   149
   150	test_that("fetch_head: accepts 'latest' and a well-formed asr_<16 hex> id", {
   151	  stub_get_json <- function(url) list(release_id = "ok")
   152	  expect_no_error(
   153	    analysis_release_zenodo_fetch_head("http://localhost:7778", "latest", http_get_json = stub_get_json)
   154	  )
   155	  expect_no_error(
   156	    analysis_release_zenodo_fetch_head(
   157	      "http://localhost:7778", "asr_deadbeefcafebabe", http_get_json = stub_get_json
   158	    )
   159	  )
   160	})
   161
   162	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   163	# fetch_head / download_bundle -- DI seams, no real network
   164	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   165
   166	test_that("fetch_head: builds the /releases/latest URL and passes the stub's JSON through", {
   167	  captured_url <- NULL
   168	  stub_get_json <- function(url) {
   169	    captured_url <<- url
   170	    list(release_id = "asr_abcdef0123456789", bundle_sha256 = "deadbeef")
   171	  }
   172	  head <- analysis_release_zenodo_fetch_head("http://localhost:7778", "latest", http_get_json = stub_get_json)
   173	  expect_identical(captured_url, "http://localhost:7778/api/analysis/releases/latest")
   174	  expect_identical(head$release_id, "asr_abcdef0123456789")
   175	})
   176
   177	test_that("fetch_head: builds the /releases/<id> URL for an explicit release_id (trailing slash tolerated)", {
   178	  captured_url <- NULL
   179	  stub_get_json <- function(url) {
   180	    captured_url <<- url
   181	    list(release_id = "asr_0000000000000000")
   182	  }
   183	  head <- analysis_release_zenodo_fetch_head(
   184	    "http://localhost:7778/", "asr_0000000000000000", http_get_json = stub_get_json
   185	  )
   186	  expect_identical(captured_url, "http://localhost:7778/api/analysis/releases/asr_0000000000000000")
   187	  expect_identical(head$release_id, "asr_0000000000000000")
   188	})
   189
   190	test_that("download_bundle: builds the /releases/<id>/bundle URL and streams the stub's content through", {
   191	  captured <- new.env()
   192	  work_dir <- withr::local_tempdir()
   193	  dest <- file.path(work_dir, "bundle.tar.gz")
   194	  stub_download <- function(url, destfile) {
   195	    assign("url", url, envir = captured)
   196	    assign("destfile", destfile, envir = captured)
   197	    writeBin(as.raw(c(1, 2, 3)), destfile)
   198	  }
   199	  result <- analysis_release_zenodo_download_bundle(
   200	    "http://localhost:7778", "asr_0000000000000000", dest, http_download = stub_download
   201	  )
   202	  expect_identical(get("url", envir = captured), "http://localhost:7778/api/analysis/releases/asr_0000000000000000/bundle")
   203	  expect_identical(get("destfile", envir = captured), dest)
   204	  expect_identical(result, dest)
   205	  expect_true(file.exists(dest))
   206	})
   207
   208	test_that("download_bundle: errors when the injected downloader produces an empty file", {
   209	  work_dir <- withr::local_tempdir()
   210	  dest <- file.path(work_dir, "empty.tar.gz")
   211	  stub_download <- function(url, destfile) {
   212	    file.create(destfile)
   213	  }
   214	  expect_error(
   215	    analysis_release_zenodo_download_bundle(
   216	      "http://localhost:7778", "asr_x", dest, http_download = stub_download
   217	    ),
   218	    "empty"
   219	  )
   220	})
   221
   222	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   223	# extract_and_verify
   224	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   225
   226	test_that("extract_and_verify: passes with a matching bundle_sha256 and matching inner checksums", {
   227	  work_dir <- withr::local_tempdir()
   228	  bundle_path <- build_fixture_bundle(work_dir)
   229	  expected_sha <- digest::digest(file = bundle_path, algo = "sha256")
   230
   231	  exdir <- file.path(work_dir, "extracted")
   232	  result_dir <- analysis_release_zenodo_extract_and_verify(bundle_path, expected_sha, exdir = exdir)
   233
   234	  expect_identical(normalizePath(result_dir), normalizePath(exdir))
   235	  expect_true(file.exists(file.path(result_dir, "a.txt")))
   236	  expect_true(file.exists(file.path(result_dir, "b.txt")))
   237	})
   238
   239	test_that("extract_and_verify: FAILS when the inner checksums.sha256 doesn't match file content", {
   240	  work_dir <- withr::local_tempdir()
   241	  bad_sha <- strrep("0", 64L)
   242	  bundle_path <- build_fixture_bundle(work_dir, a_sha_override = bad_sha)
   243	  expected_sha <- digest::digest(file = bundle_path, algo = "sha256")
   244
   245	  expect_error(
   246	    analysis_release_zenodo_extract_and_verify(
   247	      bundle_path, expected_sha, exdir = file.path(work_dir, "extracted_bad_inner")
   248	    ),
   249	    "mismatch"
   250	  )
   251	})
   252
   253	test_that("extract_and_verify: FAILS with a wrong expected_bundle_sha256 (outer verification)", {
   254	  work_dir <- withr::local_tempdir()
   255	  bundle_path <- build_fixture_bundle(work_dir)
   256
   257	  expect_error(

   323	test_that("extract_and_verify: FAILS when a TAR MEMBER path itself contains backslash traversal (Codex round-2 item 4, MEDIUM)", {
   324	  # On this Linux host, a backslash is an ordinary filename character (not a
   325	  # separator), so a file can genuinely be named with a leading `..\` --
   326	  # proving the traversal guard on the TAR MEMBER LIST (not just the
   327	  # checksums.sha256 entries) also splits on `\`, not only `/`.
   328	  work_dir <- withr::local_tempdir()
   329	  src <- file.path(work_dir, "src_backslash_member")
   330	  dir.create(src, recursive = TRUE)
   331	  weird_name <- "..\\evil_member.txt"
   332	  writeLines("payload", file.path(src, weird_name))
   333	  weird_sha <- digest::digest(file = file.path(src, weird_name), algo = "sha256")
   334	  cat(paste0(weird_sha, "  ", weird_name, "\n"), file = file.path(src, "checksums.sha256"))
   335
   336	  bundle_path <- file.path(work_dir, "bundle_backslash_member.tar.gz")
   337	  withr::with_dir(src, {
   338	    utils::tar(
   339	      tarfile = bundle_path, files = c(weird_name, "checksums.sha256"),
   340	      compression = "gzip", tar = "internal"
   341	    )
   342	  })
   343	  expected_sha <- digest::digest(file = bundle_path, algo = "sha256")
   344
   345	  expect_error(
   346	    analysis_release_zenodo_extract_and_verify(
   347	      bundle_path, expected_sha, exdir = file.path(work_dir, "extracted_backslash_member")
   348	    ),
   349	    "path traversal rejected"
   350	  )
   351	})

   353	test_that("extract_and_verify: FAILS when the bundle contains a symlinked member (Codex round-2 item 1, HIGH) -- rejected BEFORE hashing", {
   354	  # A symlinked release member must be rejected the instant it is seen in the
   355	  # EXTRACTED tree, before `digest::digest(file = ...)` (which follows
   356	  # symlinks transparently) is ever called on it. Proven here by giving the
   357	  # symlink a deliberately WRONG checksum entry (all zeros): if the rejection
   358	  # ran AFTER hashing, this would fail with a checksum "mismatch" instead --
   359	  # the assertion below pins the failure to the symlink rejection specifically.
   360	  work_dir <- withr::local_tempdir()
   361	  outside_target <- file.path(work_dir, "outside_secret.txt")
   362	  writeLines("host-readable content outside the archive", outside_target)
   363
   364	  src <- file.path(work_dir, "src_symlink_member")
   365	  dir.create(src, recursive = TRUE)
   366	  writeLines("normal file content", file.path(src, "a.txt"))
   367	  link_path <- file.path(src, "evil_link")
   368	  skip_if_not(
   369	    isTRUE(file.symlink(outside_target, link_path)),
   370	    "host does not support creating symlinks (e.g. some restricted CI runners)"
   371	  )
   372
   373	  a_sha <- digest::digest(file = file.path(src, "a.txt"), algo = "sha256")
   374	  cat(
   375	    paste0(a_sha, "  a.txt\n", strrep("0", 64L), "  evil_link\n"),
   376	    file = file.path(src, "checksums.sha256")
   377	  )
   378
   379	  bundle_path <- file.path(work_dir, "bundle_symlink_member.tar.gz")
   380	  withr::with_dir(src, {
   381	    utils::tar(
   382	      tarfile = bundle_path, files = c("a.txt", "evil_link", "checksums.sha256"),
   383	      compression = "gzip", tar = "internal"
   384	    )
   385	  })
   386	  expected_sha <- digest::digest(file = bundle_path, algo = "sha256")
   387
   388	  expect_error(
   389	    analysis_release_zenodo_extract_and_verify(
   390	      bundle_path, expected_sha, exdir = file.path(work_dir, "extracted_symlink_member")
   391	    ),
   392	    "symlinks"
   393	  )
   394	})
   395
   396	test_that("extract_and_verify: FAILS when checksums.sha256 lists the same file twice (duplicate entry, item 4)", {

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	#!/usr/bin/env Rscript
     2	# api/scripts/package-analysis-release-zenodo.R
     3	#
     4	# Thin operator CLI: fetch a PUBLISHED analysis-snapshot release (#573) over
     5	# the public SysNDD HTTP API and assemble a Zenodo-shaped dataset staging
     6	# directory + deterministic tar.gz archive, ready for
     7	# `upload-analysis-release-zenodo.R`.
     8	#
     9	# All packaging logic lives in
    10	# `api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-package.R`
    11	# (`analysis_release_zenodo_package()`, which itself guard-sources the
    12	# sibling `-docs.R` file); this script only parses flags, resolves + sources
    13	# that ONE file, calls the orchestrator, and prints the resulting paths --
    14	# mirroring the sibling
    15	# `../nddscore/scripts/package_sysndd_zenodo_dataset.py`.
    16	#
    17	# Usage (from the repo root, or from api/ -- CWD-independent, see below):
    18	#   Rscript api/scripts/package-analysis-release-zenodo.R \
    19	#     [--api-base-url http://localhost:7778] [--release-id latest] \
    20	#     [--staging-dir outputs/analysis-release-zenodo/staging] \
    21	#     [--archive-dir outputs/analysis-release-zenodo/archive] \
    22	#     [--version <override>]
    23	#
    24	# Requires: httr2, jsonlite, digest (all in api/renv.lock). No DB, no
    25	# bootstrap, no `external_proxy_budget()` -- a pure HTTP client, runnable on
    26	# any host with R (see AGENTS.md "Analysis-snapshot releases (#573)" and
    27	# `.superpowers/sdd/slice-c-scout.md` S3 for why the public API is the read
    28	# path, not the DB).
    29	#
    30	# CWD note: this script resolves
    31	# `api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-package.R` relative to ITS
    32	# OWN file location (via `sys.frame(1)$ofile`, falling back to Rscript's own
    33	# `--file=` argument -- the same idiom `capture-external-fixtures.R` uses),
    34	# so it may be invoked from any working directory; it does NOT assume CWD is
    35	# the repo root or `api/`.
    36
    37	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    38	# Resolve this script's own directory, then source the packager (functions
    39	# only -- no top-level network calls in that file, so this is safe to do
    40	# unconditionally, even when this CLI script is itself only `source()`d).
    41	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    42
    43	.package_analysis_release_zenodo_script_dir <- function() {
    44	  script_file <- tryCatch(sys.frame(1)$ofile, error = function(e) NULL)
    45	  if (is.null(script_file) || !nzchar(script_file)) {
    46	    full_args <- commandArgs(trailingOnly = FALSE)
    47	    file_arg <- grep("^--file=", full_args, value = TRUE)
    48	    script_file <- if (length(file_arg) >= 1) sub("^--file=", "", file_arg[1]) else "."
    49	  }
    50	  dirname(normalizePath(script_file, mustWork = FALSE))
    51	}
    52
    53	.package_analysis_release_zenodo_script_dir_value <- .package_analysis_release_zenodo_script_dir()
    54	source(
    55	  file.path(
    56	    .package_analysis_release_zenodo_script_dir_value, "..", "functions",
    57	    "analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-package.R"
    58	  ),
    59	  local = FALSE
    60	)
    61
    62	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    63	# CLI arg parsing -- manual commandArgs() flag loop (repo convention; no
    64	# optparse dependency, see `verify-endpoints.R`).
    65	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    66
    67	#' Parse + run the packaging CLI. Wrapped in a function (rather than bare
    68	#' top-level code) so the file can be `source()`d for its function
    69	#' definitions alone -- see the `sys.nframe() == 0L` main-guard at the
    70	#' bottom of this file, which only calls this when the script is run
    71	#' directly (`Rscript package-analysis-release-zenodo.R`), never when
    72	#' `source()`d (e.g. by a test).
    73	#' Write/overwrite the stable `outputs/analysis-release-zenodo/latest.env`
    74	#' pointer file the `analysis-release-zenodo-upload-draft` Make target reads
    75	#' to find this run's content-addressed archive (releases are `asr_<16
    76	#' hex>`-named, not date-versioned, so the Makefile cannot hardcode a
    77	#' filename). Fixed, CWD-relative location regardless of `--staging-dir`/
    78	#' `--archive-dir` overrides -- deliberately NOT under `staging_dir`/
    79	#' `archive_dir` so the marker's location never depends on those flags.
    80	#' Paths are written `normalizePath()`-resolved (absolute) so the marker is
    81	#' still correct if the upload step runs from a different CWD than the
    82	#' package step.
    83	.write_package_analysis_release_zenodo_marker <- function(result) {
    84	  marker_path <- "outputs/analysis-release-zenodo/latest.env"
    85	  dir.create(dirname(marker_path), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
    86	  cat(
    87	    sprintf(
    88	      "ARCHIVE_PATH=%s\nMETADATA_PATH=%s\nRELEASE_ID=%s\n",
    89	      normalizePath(result$archive_path, mustWork = FALSE),
    90	      normalizePath(result$zenodo_metadata_path, mustWork = FALSE),
    91	      result$release_id
    92	    ),
    93	    file = marker_path
    94	  )
    95	  marker_path
    96	}
    97
    98	run_package_analysis_release_zenodo_cli <- function() {
    99	  args <- commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE)
   100
   101	  api_base_url <- Sys.getenv("SYSNDD_API_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:7778")
   102	  release_id <- "latest"
   103	  staging_dir <- "outputs/analysis-release-zenodo/staging"
   104	  archive_dir <- "outputs/analysis-release-zenodo/archive"
   105	  version <- NULL
   106
   107	  if (length(args) > 0) {
   108	    for (i in seq_along(args)) {
   109	      if (args[i] == "--api-base-url" && i < length(args)) {
   110	        api_base_url <- args[i + 1]
   111	      } else if (args[i] == "--release-id" && i < length(args)) {
   112	        release_id <- args[i + 1]
   113	      } else if (args[i] == "--staging-dir" && i < length(args)) {
   114	        staging_dir <- args[i + 1]
   115	      } else if (args[i] == "--archive-dir" && i < length(args)) {
   116	        archive_dir <- args[i + 1]
   117	      } else if (args[i] == "--version" && i < length(args)) {
   118	        version <- args[i + 1]
   119	      }
   120	    }
   121	  }
   122
   123	  message(sprintf(
   124	    "[package-analysis-release-zenodo] api_base_url=%s release_id=%s staging_dir=%s archive_dir=%s",
   125	    api_base_url, release_id, staging_dir, archive_dir
   126	  ))
   127
   128	  result <- analysis_release_zenodo_package(
   129	    api_base_url = api_base_url,
   130	    release_id = release_id,
   131	    staging_dir = staging_dir,
   132	    archive_dir = archive_dir,
   133	    version = version
   134	  )
   135
   136	  marker_path <- .write_package_analysis_release_zenodo_marker(result)
   137
   138	  cat(sprintf("Release ID:           %s\n", result$release_id))
   139	  cat(sprintf("Staging dir:          %s\n", result$staging_dir))
   140	  cat(sprintf("Archive path:         %s\n", result$archive_path))
   141	  cat(sprintf("Archive sha256 path:  %s\n", result$archive_sha256_path))
   142	  cat(sprintf("Zenodo metadata path: %s\n", result$zenodo_metadata_path))
   143	  cat(sprintf("Marker file:          %s\n", marker_path))
   144	  cat(sprintf(
   145	    "\nNext: make analysis-release-zenodo-upload-draft (or: Rscript api/scripts/upload-analysis-release-zenodo.R --archive %s --metadata %s --release-id %s)\n",
   146	    result$archive_path, result$zenodo_metadata_path, result$release_id
   147	  ))
   148
   149	  invisible(result)
   150	}
   151
   152	# Main guard: `sys.nframe()` is 0 only at the true top level of a directly
   153	# `Rscript`-invoked file; a `source()`d file (e.g. from a test) always adds
   154	# at least one frame, so this line never fires under `source()`. This check
   155	# MUST stay at top level (not inside a function) -- see AGENTS.md-adjacent
   156	# `.superpowers/sdd/task-c2-report.md` for the empirical verification.
   157	if (sys.nframe() == 0L) run_package_analysis_release_zenodo_cli()
     1	#!/usr/bin/env Rscript
     2	# api/scripts/upload-analysis-release-zenodo.R
     3	#
     4	# Thin operator CLI: upload a packaged analysis-snapshot release archive
     5	# (#573; output of `package-analysis-release-zenodo.R`) to a Zenodo
     6	# deposition -- get-or-create -> set metadata -> stream archive to bucket ->
     7	# (optional, double-gated) publish -- then, opt-in, PATCH the resulting
     8	# DOI/record provenance back onto the SysNDD release head.
     9	#
    10	# All upload/record-back logic lives in
    11	# `api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-upload.R`; this script
    12	# only parses flags, sources that file, calls the orchestrator, and prints
    13	# results -- mirroring the sibling
    14	# `../nddscore/scripts/upload_sysndd_zenodo_dataset.py`.
    15	#
    16	# Usage (from the repo root, or from api/ -- CWD-independent, see below):
    17	#   Rscript api/scripts/upload-analysis-release-zenodo.R \
    18	#     --archive <path/to/archive.tar.gz> --metadata <path/to/zenodo_metadata.json> \
    19	#     [--sandbox] [--deposition-id <id>] [--publish --confirm-publish] \
    20	#     [--record-doi --release-id <asr_...>] [--api-base-url <SysNDD API base>]
    21	#
    22	# ZENODO_TOKEN is READ FROM THE ENVIRONMENT ONLY -- there is deliberately no
    23	# `--token` flag. A CLI flag would leak the token into shell history and
    24	# `ps`/`/proc/<pid>/cmdline` argv on any multi-user host; the underlying
    25	# `analysis_release_zenodo_upload()` function still accepts a `token`
    26	# parameter for tests/programmatic callers, but this CLI wrapper only ever
    27	# reads `Sys.getenv("ZENODO_TOKEN")`.
    28	#
    29	# Publish safety interlock: `--publish` alone is REFUSED -- both `--publish`
    30	# AND `--confirm-publish` must be passed, or the run stops before any HTTP
    31	# call is made (`analysis_release_zenodo_require_publish_confirmation()`).
    32	# Without `--publish`, the archive is uploaded to a Zenodo DRAFT only, for
    33	# manual review before publishing.
    34	#
    35	# DOI record-back is OPT-IN and requires BOTH `--record-doi` AND the
    36	# `SYSNDD_ADMIN_TOKEN` env var (a pre-minted SysNDD Administrator bearer
    37	# token) to be set, plus `--release-id` and a successfully PUBLISHED Zenodo
    38	# DOI. Absent any of those, this script NEVER calls the SysNDD admin PATCH
    39	# endpoint automatically -- it prints the exact manual `curl` command instead
    40	# so the operator can record it by hand. A DRAFT upload (no `--publish`, or
    41	# `--publish` without `--confirm-publish`) never gets a populated PATCH
    42	# command either way -- only post-publication instructions are printed,
    43	# because a draft DOI is not final and the record-back rule is
    44	# published-only (see `analysis_release_zenodo_print_doi_record_back()`).
    45	# `--release-id` is validated (`^asr_[0-9a-f]{16}$`) before it is ever placed
    46	# into the admin URL or the printed command.
    47	#
    48	# Requires: httr2, jsonlite (api/renv.lock). No DB, no bootstrap, no
    49	# `external_proxy_budget()` -- see AGENTS.md "Analysis-snapshot releases
    50	# (#573)" and `.superpowers/sdd/slice-c-scout.md`.
    51	#
    52	# CWD note: resolves `api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-
    53	# upload.R` relative to ITS OWN file location (same idiom as
    54	# `package-analysis-release-zenodo.R` / `capture-external-fixtures.R`), so it
    55	# may be invoked from any working directory.
    56
    57	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    58	# Resolve this script's own directory, then source the upload/record-back
    59	# functions (function definitions only -- no top-level network calls in that
    60	# file, so this is safe to do unconditionally, even when this CLI script is
    61	# itself only `source()`d).
    62	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    63
    64	.upload_analysis_release_zenodo_script_dir <- function() {
    65	  script_file <- tryCatch(sys.frame(1)$ofile, error = function(e) NULL)
    66	  if (is.null(script_file) || !nzchar(script_file)) {
    67	    full_args <- commandArgs(trailingOnly = FALSE)
    68	    file_arg <- grep("^--file=", full_args, value = TRUE)
    69	    script_file <- if (length(file_arg) >= 1) sub("^--file=", "", file_arg[1]) else "."
    70	  }
    71	  dirname(normalizePath(script_file, mustWork = FALSE))
    72	}
    73
    74	.upload_analysis_release_zenodo_script_dir_value <- .upload_analysis_release_zenodo_script_dir()
    75	source(
    76	  file.path(
    77	    .upload_analysis_release_zenodo_script_dir_value, "..", "functions",
    78	    "analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-upload.R"
    79	  ),
    80	  local = FALSE
    81	)
    82
    83	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    84	# CLI arg parsing -- manual commandArgs() flag loop (repo convention; no
    85	# optparse dependency, see `verify-endpoints.R`).
    86	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    87
    88	#' Parse + run the upload CLI. Wrapped in a function (rather than bare
    89	#' top-level code) so the file can be `source()`d for its function
    90	#' definitions alone -- see the `sys.nframe() == 0L` main-guard at the
    91	#' bottom of this file, which only calls this when the script is run
    92	#' directly (`Rscript upload-analysis-release-zenodo.R`), never when
    93	#' `source()`d (e.g. by a test).
    94	run_upload_analysis_release_zenodo_cli <- function() {
    95	  args <- commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE)
    96
    97	  archive <- NULL
    98	  metadata <- NULL
    99	  # ZENODO_TOKEN is env-only -- see the "no --token flag" note in the file
   100	  # header. There is no CLI flag to set/override it.
   101	  token <- Sys.getenv("ZENODO_TOKEN", "")
   102	  sandbox <- FALSE
   103	  deposition_id <- NULL
   104	  publish <- FALSE
   105	  confirm_publish <- FALSE
   106	  record_doi <- FALSE
   107	  release_id <- NULL
   108	  api_base_url <- Sys.getenv("SYSNDD_API_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:7778")
   109
   110	  if (length(args) > 0) {
   111	    for (i in seq_along(args)) {
   112	      if (args[i] == "--archive" && i < length(args)) {
   113	        archive <- args[i + 1]
   114	      } else if (args[i] == "--metadata" && i < length(args)) {
   115	        metadata <- args[i + 1]
   116	      } else if (args[i] == "--sandbox") {
   117	        sandbox <- TRUE
   118	      } else if (args[i] == "--deposition-id" && i < length(args)) {
   119	        deposition_id <- args[i + 1]
   120	      } else if (args[i] == "--publish") {
   121	        publish <- TRUE
   122	      } else if (args[i] == "--confirm-publish") {
   123	        confirm_publish <- TRUE
   124	      } else if (args[i] == "--record-doi") {
   125	        record_doi <- TRUE
   126	      } else if (args[i] == "--release-id" && i < length(args)) {
   127	        release_id <- args[i + 1]
   128	      } else if (args[i] == "--api-base-url" && i < length(args)) {
   129	        api_base_url <- args[i + 1]
   130	      }
   131	    }
   132	  }
   133
   134	  if (is.null(archive) || !nzchar(archive)) {
   135	    stop("--archive is required", call. = FALSE)
   136	  }
   137	  if (is.null(metadata) || !nzchar(metadata)) {
   138	    stop("--metadata is required", call. = FALSE)
   139	  }
   140
   141	  result <- analysis_release_zenodo_upload(
   142	    archive_path = archive,
   143	    metadata_path = metadata,
   144	    token = token,
   145	    sandbox = sandbox,
   146	    deposition_id = deposition_id,
   147	    publish = publish,
   148	    confirm_publish = confirm_publish
   149	  )
   150
   151	  cat(sprintf("Zenodo deposition id: %s\n", result$deposition_id))
   152	  cat(sprintf(
   153	    "Zenodo reserved DOI:  %s\n",
   154	    if (is.na(result$reserved_doi)) "not returned" else result$reserved_doi
   155	  ))
   156	  cat(sprintf(
   157	    "Zenodo draft URL:     %s\n",
   158	    if (is.na(result$draft_url)) "not returned" else result$draft_url
   159	  ))
   160
   161	  if (isTRUE(result$published)) {
   162	    cat(sprintf("Published Zenodo DOI: %s\n", result$version_doi))
   163	    cat(sprintf("Published Zenodo URL: %s\n", result$record_url))
   164	  } else {
   165	    cat("Draft uploaded only. Review in Zenodo before publishing.\n")
   166	  }
   167
   168	  # Item 3 (MEDIUM, Codex round 2): `analysis_release_zenodo_print_doi_record_back()`
   169	  # (in the functions file, not duplicated here) prints ONLY post-publication
   170	  # instructions for a draft upload -- never a populated PATCH command.
   171	  analysis_release_zenodo_print_doi_record_back(result, release_id, api_base_url, record_doi)
   172
   173	  invisible(result)
   174	}
   175
   176	# Main guard: `sys.nframe()` is 0 only at the true top level of a directly
   177	# `Rscript`-invoked file; a `source()`d file (e.g. from a test) always adds
   178	# at least one frame, so this line never fires under `source()`. This check
   179	# MUST stay at top level (not inside a function).
   180	if (sys.nframe() == 0L) run_upload_analysis_release_zenodo_cli()

codex
I’ve confirmed the round‑2 control flow is present: the extracted-tree symlink check runs directly after extraction, and the DOI print path exits for drafts before constructing the manual command. I’m now pressure-testing the shared guard’s edge cases and the helper call sites rather than relying on the new comments/tests alone.

commit b236b1bd9a8bc40310fd5c05389744fdc9c7742e
Author: Bernt Popp <bernt.popp@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Jul 19 13:02:03 2026 +0200

    fix(api): harden analysis-release Zenodo scripts against Codex round-2 findings (#573)

    Round-2 adversarial review found four adjacent issues in the Slice C
    Zenodo operator scripts, all confirmed real:

    - HIGH: reject symlinks in the EXTRACTED bundle tree immediately after
      untar() and before any checksum hashing or staging copy, so a
      symlinked release member can no longer be followed to pull
      host-readable content into the archive.
    - HIGH: validate --release-id (^asr_[0-9a-f]{16}$) on the upload/DOI
      path too, before building the admin PATCH URL or the printed curl
      command -- previously only the package path validated, leaving a
      copy/paste command-injection vector. Extracted the shared validator
      into a new analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-common.R guard-sourced by
      both paths, and shQuote() the interpolated values as defense in
      depth.
    - MEDIUM: never print a populated PATCH command for a draft upload
      (published-only rule); print post-publication instructions only.
    - MEDIUM: traversal check now splits on backslash too and rejects
      leading-backslash/UNC/drive-letter paths, not just POSIX '/'.

    Split the round-2 upload tests into a sibling
    test-unit-analysis-release-zenodo-upload-doi-safety.R to keep every
    touched file under the repo's 600-line ceiling.

 AGENTS.md                                          |   2 +-
 .../analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-common.R      |  75 +++++++
 .../analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-upload.R      | 163 +++++++++++++-
 .../analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-verify.R      | 182 ++++++++++------
 api/scripts/upload-analysis-release-zenodo.R       |  60 +-----
 ...nit-analysis-release-zenodo-upload-doi-safety.R | 239 +++++++++++++++++++++
 .../test-unit-analysis-release-zenodo-upload.R     |  12 +-
 .../test-unit-analysis-release-zenodo-verify.R     | 103 +++++++++
 8 files changed, 703 insertions(+), 133 deletions(-)

Author:     Bernt Popp <bernt.popp@gmail.com>
AuthorDate: Sun Jul 19 13:02:03 2026 +0200
Commit:     Bernt Popp <bernt.popp@gmail.com>
CommitDate: Sun Jul 19 13:02:03 2026 +0200

    fix(api): harden analysis-release Zenodo scripts against Codex round-2 findings (#573)

    Round-2 adversarial review found four adjacent issues in the Slice C
    Zenodo operator scripts, all confirmed real:

    - HIGH: reject symlinks in the EXTRACTED bundle tree immediately after
      untar() and before any checksum hashing or staging copy, so a
      symlinked release member can no longer be followed to pull
      host-readable content into the archive.
    - HIGH: validate --release-id (^asr_[0-9a-f]{16}$) on the upload/DOI
      path too, before building the admin PATCH URL or the printed curl
      command -- previously only the package path validated, leaving a
      copy/paste command-injection vector. Extracted the shared validator
      into a new analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-common.R guard-sourced by
      both paths, and shQuote() the interpolated values as defense in
      depth.
    - MEDIUM: never print a populated PATCH command for a draft upload
      (published-only rule); print post-publication instructions only.
    - MEDIUM: traversal check now splits on backslash too and rejects
      leading-backslash/UNC/drive-letter paths, not just POSIX '/'.

    Split the round-2 upload tests into a sibling
    test-unit-analysis-release-zenodo-upload-doi-safety.R to keep every
    touched file under the repo's 600-line ceiling.

diff --git a/api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-common.R b/api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-common.R
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..520d8d1e
--- /dev/null
+++ b/api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-common.R
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+# api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-common.R
+#
+# Tiny shared helpers for the analysis-snapshot RELEASE Zenodo operator
+# scripts (#573 Slice C, Codex round-2 hardening item 2 -- HIGH): the `%||%`
+# NULL-coalesce helper and the STRICT release-id shape validator
+# (`.analysis_release_zenodo_assert_valid_release_id()`).
+#
+# Guard-sourced by BOTH the package/verify pair
+# (`analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-verify.R`, itself guard-sourced by
+# `-package.R`) AND the upload/DOI file
+# (`analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-upload.R`) so the validator is defined
+# exactly once and every operator-script path -- package AND upload/DOI --
+# rejects a malformed release id identically, before it is ever placed into a
+# URL or a printed shell command.
+#
+# Extracted here (rather than left duplicated only in `-verify.R`, where the
+# round-1 guard originally lived) because Codex round 2 found the upload/DOI
+# path (`record_doi()`/`manual_doi_command()`, and the CLI's `--release-id`
+# flag) built an admin PATCH URL AND a printed `curl` command straight from
+# an UNVALIDATED `--release-id` -- a value like `asr_x' ; rm -rf ~ ; #`
+# becomes a copy/paste command-injection payload once printed inside a
+# single-quoted shell command, and a `../` value alters the URL path.
+#
+# NOT API-runtime code: not registered in `bootstrap/load_modules.R`. Kept
+# well under the repo's 600-line soft ceiling on purpose -- this file only
+# grows if a THIRD operator-script path needs the same guard.
+
+.analysis_release_zenodo_common_loaded <- TRUE
+
+`%||%` <- function(a, b) {
+  if (is.null(a) || length(a) == 0) {
+    b
+  } else {
+    a
+  }
+}
+
+# A published release id is `"asr_" + 16 lowercase hex chars`
+# (`analysis_release_id()` in `analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R`). This is
+# the ONLY shape ever allowed to become a filename, path component, URL
+# segment, or a value interpolated into a printed shell command.
+.ANALYSIS_RELEASE_ZENODO_ID_PATTERN <- "^asr_[0-9a-f]{16}$"
+
+#' Stop unless `release_id` is exactly `"latest"` (only when
+#' `allow_latest = TRUE`, i.e. a caller-supplied REQUEST arg) or a
+#' well-formed `asr_<16 lowercase hex>` id.
+#'
+#' Shared by BOTH:
+#' - the package/verify path (`analysis_release_zenodo_fetch_head()`'s
+#'   REQUEST arg, and defense-in-depth on the RESOLVED id from the release
+#'   head in `analysis_release_zenodo_package()`) -- this is the value that
+#'   becomes `<release_id>.tar.gz` and the `RELEASE_ID=` marker line;
+#' - the upload/DOI path (`analysis_release_zenodo_record_doi()`,
+#'   `analysis_release_zenodo_manual_doi_command()`, called before EITHER
+#'   builds the admin PATCH URL or the printed `curl` command from the
+#'   CLI's `--release-id`).
+#'
+#' Any other value (`../evil`, an id containing a quote, `;`, a newline, or
+#' other shell metacharacters, ...) stops loudly here, before it is ever used
+#' as a path/filename/marker/URL segment or interpolated into a command.
+#'
+#' @return `release_id` (as a length-1 character), invisibly, on success.
+.analysis_release_zenodo_assert_valid_release_id <- function(release_id, allow_latest = FALSE) {
+  value <- as.character(release_id)[[1]]
+  if (isTRUE(allow_latest) && identical(value, "latest")) {
+    return(invisible(value))
+  }
+  if (!grepl(.ANALYSIS_RELEASE_ZENODO_ID_PATTERN, value)) {
+    stop(sprintf(
+      "Invalid analysis-snapshot release id %s: expected %s'asr_<16 lowercase hex>'",
+      shQuote(value), if (isTRUE(allow_latest)) "'latest' or " else ""
+    ), call. = FALSE)
+  }
+  invisible(value)
+}
diff --git a/api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-upload.R b/api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-upload.R
index c89b4999..d0ef7f44 100644
--- a/api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-upload.R
+++ b/api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-upload.R
@@ -8,50 +8,86 @@
 # `bootstrap/load_modules.R`. The C2 CLI wrapper
 # (`api/scripts/upload-analysis-release-zenodo.R`) sources this file
 # directly; tests use `source_api_file(..., local = FALSE)`.
 #
 # Mirrors the sibling `../nddscore/scripts/upload_sysndd_zenodo_dataset.py`
 # flow (deposition get-or-create -> set metadata -> PUT archive to bucket ->
 # optional publish), translated Python -> R.
 #
 # Every HTTP boundary is an injectable seam parameter with a real `httr2`
 # default (the `.analysis_release_zenodo_http_*` functions below), so unit
 # tests inject plain stub closures -- no mocking library, no real network.
 # Per AGENTS.md ("External-budget guard: Slice C scripts ARE EXEMPT" in the
 # planning scout, and the documented `publication-functions.R`/
 # `nddscore-release-source.R` precedent), one-shot operator scripts are
 # outside `external_proxy_budget()` -- plain `httr2::req_timeout()` literals
 # are used directly.
 #
 # Publish is DOUBLE-gated: `analysis_release_zenodo_require_publish_confirmation()`
 # stops unless BOTH `publish` and `confirm_publish` are set -- the orchestrator
 # is draft-only by default. DOI record-back to the SysNDD admin API is OPT-IN
-# and lives OUTSIDE this pure orchestrator (it is invoked by the CLI script,
-# Deliverable 3), so this file stays free of SysNDD-admin coupling and easy to
-# test in isolation.
+# (`analysis_release_zenodo_record_doi()`/`analysis_release_zenodo_manual_doi_command()`/
+# `analysis_release_zenodo_print_doi_record_back()` below); the CLI script
+# only wires flags/env into the latter, so this file stays the single place
+# that decides WHEN a DOI is recorded/printed and is easy to test in
+# isolation.
+#
+# Codex round-2 hardening (item 2, HIGH): the DOI/record-back path takes an
+# operator-supplied `--release-id` and both builds an admin PATCH URL from it
+# AND interpolates it into a printed single-quoted `curl` command -- so an
+# unvalidated value is a path/URL-injection AND a copy/paste
+# command-injection vector. Every DOI URL/command builder below calls the
+# shared `.analysis_release_zenodo_assert_valid_release_id()` (guard-sourced
+# from the sibling `analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-common.R`, same
+# validator the package/verify path already used) FIRST, and
+# `manual_doi_command()` additionally `shQuote()`s every interpolated value
+# as defense in depth.

-`%||%` <- function(a, b) {
-  if (is.null(a) || length(a) == 0) {
-    b
-  } else {
-    a
+if (!exists(".analysis_release_zenodo_common_loaded", mode = "logical")) {
+  # Same self-locating guard-source idiom as `-package.R`'s docs/verify
+  # blocks and `-verify.R`'s own common guard block: resolves this file's
+  # own directory from the active source() frame so the sibling common file
+  # loads regardless of cwd or how this file was sourced (directly by the
+  # CLI script, or by `source_api_file()` in tests).
+  .analysis_release_zenodo_common_self_dir <- local({
+    ofiles <- Filter(Negate(is.null), lapply(sys.frames(), function(f) f$ofile))
+    if (length(ofiles) > 0) dirname(normalizePath(ofiles[[length(ofiles)]])) else NULL
+  })
+  .analysis_release_zenodo_common_candidates <- c(
+    if (!is.null(.analysis_release_zenodo_common_self_dir)) {
+      file.path(.analysis_release_zenodo_common_self_dir, "analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-common.R")
+    },
+    "functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-common.R",
+    "/app/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-common.R"
+  )
+  for (.analysis_release_zenodo_common_path in .analysis_release_zenodo_common_candidates) {
+    if (file.exists(.analysis_release_zenodo_common_path)) {
+      # local = TRUE: evaluate into THIS call's parent frame (same reasoning
+      # as `-package.R`'s guard blocks).
+      source(.analysis_release_zenodo_common_path, local = TRUE)
+      break
+    }
   }
+  rm(
+    .analysis_release_zenodo_common_self_dir, .analysis_release_zenodo_common_candidates,
+    .analysis_release_zenodo_common_path
+  )
 }

 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
 # Zenodo API base URLs + publish safety interlock
 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

 .ANALYSIS_RELEASE_ZENODO_API_PROD <- "https://zenodo.org/api"
 .ANALYSIS_RELEASE_ZENODO_API_SANDBOX <- "https://sandbox.zenodo.org/api"

 #' Resolve the Zenodo API base URL for the requested environment.
 analysis_release_zenodo_resolve_api <- function(sandbox = FALSE) {
   if (isTRUE(sandbox)) {
     .ANALYSIS_RELEASE_ZENODO_API_SANDBOX
   } else {
     .ANALYSIS_RELEASE_ZENODO_API_PROD
   }
 }

 #' Stop unless BOTH `publish` and `confirm_publish` are set. A `publish`
 #' request without explicit confirmation is refused -- draft-only is the
@@ -194,68 +230,175 @@ analysis_release_zenodo_publish_deposition <- function(
     if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0) {
       return(FALSE)
     }
     scalar <- value[[1]]
     if (is.na(scalar)) {
       return(FALSE)
     }
     nzchar(trimws(as.character(scalar)))
   }, doi_fields)
 }

 #' PATCH the four Zenodo/DOI provenance fields onto a published release head.
 #' Additive-only: forwards ONLY the supplied non-empty fields, matching the
 #' admin endpoint's "an omitted field is left unchanged, never nulled out"
 #' contract (see AGENTS.md "Analysis-snapshot releases (#573)").
 #'
 #' @param sysndd_api_base_url Base URL of the SysNDD API.
 #' @param admin_token A pre-minted SysNDD Administrator bearer token
 #'   (`SYSNDD_ADMIN_TOKEN`). Distinct from the Zenodo `token` used elsewhere
 #'   in this file.
+#' @param release_id Validated via
+#'   `.analysis_release_zenodo_assert_valid_release_id()` BEFORE the admin
+#'   PATCH URL is built (Codex round-2 item 2, HIGH) -- an invalid id
+#'   (`../evil`, a quote/`;`/newline-shaped value, ...) stops here, before it
+#'   is ever placed into the URL.
 #' @param patch Function(method, url, token, body = NULL) -> parsed JSON list.
 #'   Injectable seam; defaults to the real httr2 call.
 analysis_release_zenodo_record_doi <- function(
     sysndd_api_base_url, admin_token, release_id, doi_fields,
     patch = .analysis_release_zenodo_http_json) {
   base_url <- sub("/+$", "", as.character(sysndd_api_base_url)[[1]])
   release_id <- as.character(release_id)[[1]]
+  .analysis_release_zenodo_assert_valid_release_id(release_id, allow_latest = FALSE)
   url <- paste0(base_url, "/api/admin/analysis/releases/", release_id, "/doi")
   fields <- .analysis_release_zenodo_doi_non_empty_fields(doi_fields)
   patch("PATCH", url, admin_token, body = fields)
 }

 #' Build the exact `curl -X PATCH ...` command an operator can run by hand to
 #' record DOI/record provenance when `--record-doi` was not opted into (the
 #' default). Never executed automatically.
+#'
+#' Codex round-2 item 2 (HIGH): `release_id` is validated via
+#' `.analysis_release_zenodo_assert_valid_release_id()` BEFORE the URL or
+#' command string is built -- an invalid id stops here rather than becoming a
+#' path-altering URL segment or a copy/paste command-injection payload once
+#' printed. Defense in depth: the resolved URL and the JSON body are each
+#' `shQuote()`d (POSIX `sh` quoting -- wraps in single quotes and escapes any
+#' embedded single quote), so even a doi_fields VALUE containing a quote,
+#' `;`, or a newline cannot break out of the single-quoted `curl` arguments.
 analysis_release_zenodo_manual_doi_command <- function(sysndd_api_base_url, release_id, doi_fields) {
   base_url <- sub("/+$", "", as.character(sysndd_api_base_url)[[1]])
   release_id <- as.character(release_id)[[1]]
+  .analysis_release_zenodo_assert_valid_release_id(release_id, allow_latest = FALSE)
   url <- paste0(base_url, "/api/admin/analysis/releases/", release_id, "/doi")
   fields <- .analysis_release_zenodo_doi_non_empty_fields(doi_fields)
   body_json <- as.character(jsonlite::toJSON(fields, auto_unbox = TRUE))

   paste0(
-    "curl -X PATCH '", url, "' ",
+    "curl -X PATCH ", shQuote(url), " ",
     "-H 'Authorization: Bearer <SYSNDD_ADMIN_TOKEN>' ",
     "-H 'Content-Type: application/json' ",
-    "-d '", body_json, "'"
+    "-d ", shQuote(body_json)
+  )
+}
+
+# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
+# DOI record-back print step -- the CLI-facing decision of WHEN to
+# auto-record vs. print a manual command vs. print instructions-only.
+# Lives here (not in the CLI script) so it is directly unit-testable via
+# `source_api_file()`, the same convention as the rest of this file.
+# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
+
+#' Print the (opt-in) DOI record-back step after `analysis_release_zenodo_upload()`
+#' completes. Never calls the SysNDD admin endpoint unless the operator
+#' explicitly asked for it (`--record-doi`) AND supplied credentials
+#' (`SYSNDD_ADMIN_TOKEN`) AND the run reached a real, published DOI.
+#'
+#' Codex round-2 hardening:
+#' - item 2 (HIGH): `release_id` is validated via
+#'   `.analysis_release_zenodo_assert_valid_release_id()` immediately after
+#'   the "no id supplied" short-circuit and BEFORE any URL or command is
+#'   built from it (`record_doi()`/`manual_doi_command()` also validate
+#'   internally -- this is defense in depth, not the only gate).
+#' - item 3 (MEDIUM): when the upload was a DRAFT (`result$published` is
+#'   FALSE), this prints ONLY post-publication instructions -- never a
+#'   ready-to-run PATCH command populated with draft values, which would let
+#'   an operator record a draft DOI and violate the locked published-only
+#'   rule (the automatic `--record-doi` path already gated on `published`;
+#'   the PRINTED fallback command previously did not).
+#'
+#' @param result The list returned by `analysis_release_zenodo_upload()`.
+#' @param release_id Operator-supplied `--release-id` (or `NULL`).
+#' @param api_base_url Base URL of the SysNDD API.
+#' @param record_doi Whether `--record-doi` was passed.
+#' @param printer Function(...) used for output; defaults to `cat`.
+#'   Injectable seam so tests can capture output without `capture.output()`.
+#' @param record_doi_fn Function with `analysis_release_zenodo_record_doi()`'s
+#'   signature. Injectable seam (mirrors every other HTTP boundary in this
+#'   file) so tests can exercise the automatic-record branch without a real
+#'   network call.
+analysis_release_zenodo_print_doi_record_back <- function(
+    result, release_id, api_base_url, record_doi, printer = cat,
+    record_doi_fn = analysis_release_zenodo_record_doi) {
+  have_release_id <- !is.null(release_id) && nzchar(as.character(release_id)[[1]])
+  if (!have_release_id) {
+    printer("\nNo --release-id supplied -- pass one to record or print a DOI record-back command.\n")
+    return(invisible(NULL))
+  }
+
+  release_id <- as.character(release_id)[[1]]
+  .analysis_release_zenodo_assert_valid_release_id(release_id, allow_latest = FALSE)
+
+  if (!isTRUE(result$published)) {
+    printer(
+      "\nDraft uploaded (not published) -- a draft DOI is never recorded (published-only ",
+      "rule). Publish first with --publish --confirm-publish, then re-run with ",
+      "--record-doi (and SYSNDD_ADMIN_TOKEN set), or record the DOI by hand once ",
+      "the deposition is published.\n",
+      sep = ""
+    )
+    return(invisible(NULL))
+  }
+
+  doi_fields <- list(
+    zenodo_record_id = as.character(result$deposition_id),
+    zenodo_record_url = result$record_url,
+    version_doi = result$version_doi,
+    concept_doi = result$concept_doi
   )
+  have_published_doi <- !is.na(result$version_doi) && nzchar(as.character(result$version_doi))
+  admin_token <- Sys.getenv("SYSNDD_ADMIN_TOKEN", "")
+
+  if (isTRUE(record_doi) && nzchar(admin_token) && have_published_doi) {
+    updated <- record_doi_fn(
+      sysndd_api_base_url = api_base_url,
+      admin_token = admin_token,
+      release_id = release_id,
+      doi_fields = doi_fields
+    )
+    printer("\nDOI recorded on the SysNDD release head:\n")
+    printer(sprintf("  release_id:        %s\n", updated$release_id %||% release_id))
+    printer(sprintf("  version_doi:       %s\n", updated$version_doi %||% doi_fields$version_doi))
+    printer(sprintf("  zenodo_record_url: %s\n", updated$zenodo_record_url %||% doi_fields$zenodo_record_url))
+  } else {
+    printer(
+      "\nDOI not recorded automatically",
+      if (!have_published_doi) " (no published DOI)" else "",
+      ". Run with --record-doi and SYSNDD_ADMIN_TOKEN set, ",
+      "or record it by hand:\n",
+      sep = ""
+    )
+    printer(analysis_release_zenodo_manual_doi_command(api_base_url, release_id, doi_fields), "\n")
+  }
+  invisible(NULL)
 }

 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
 # Orchestrator -- mirrors the Python sibling's `main()` flow. Pure w.r.t.
 # SysNDD: it never calls `analysis_release_zenodo_record_doi()` itself (the
 # CLI script does that, after this returns).
 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

 #' Upload a packaged Zenodo archive: get-or-create deposition -> set metadata
 #' -> stream archive to bucket -> (if `publish`) publish. Draft-only unless
 #' BOTH `publish` and `confirm_publish` are set (enforced first, via
 #' `analysis_release_zenodo_require_publish_confirmation()`).
 #'
 #' @return list(deposition_id, reserved_doi, draft_url, published (bool),
 #'   version_doi, concept_doi, record_url). `version_doi`/`concept_doi`/
 #'   `record_url` are `NA_character_` unless `published` is TRUE.
 analysis_release_zenodo_upload <- function(
     archive_path,
     metadata_path,
     token,
diff --git a/api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-verify.R b/api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-verify.R
index 43f03ecd..039ee5f6 100644
--- a/api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-verify.R
+++ b/api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-verify.R
@@ -1,115 +1,120 @@
 # api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-verify.R
 #
 # Fetch/download/extract-verify helpers AND the safety validator for the
 # analysis-snapshot RELEASE Zenodo packager (#573 Slice C). Extracted from
 # `analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-package.R` (which guard-sources this
 # file) to keep both files under the repo's 600-line soft ceiling -- mirrors
 # the sibling `analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-docs.R` split and the
 # `comparisons-functions.R` / `comparisons-parsers.R` precedent.
 #
 # This file is the hardening surface for a script that publishes public data
 # and moves an operator-supplied filesystem path: release-id validation
 # (path/filename/marker-injection guard), the extracted-bundle
 # coverage/traversal checks, and the staging-tree safety validator
 # (case-insensitive forbidden-name matching, a file-type allowlist, and a
 # symlink rejection) all live here.
 #
 # Depends on `%||%`, defined in the sibling `analysis-snapshot-release-
 # zenodo-package.R` (which always sources this file, never the other way
 # around) -- resolved lazily at CALL time via the shared sourcing
 # environment, not at source time, so definition order across the two files
-# does not matter (same idiom as the `-docs.R` sibling).
+# does not matter (same idiom as the `-docs.R` sibling). Also guard-sources
+# the sibling `analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-common.R` below (release-id
+# validator, Codex round-2 item 2) so this file works standalone even if a
+# caller sources it directly instead of via `-package.R`.

 .analysis_release_zenodo_verify_loaded <- TRUE

+if (!exists(".analysis_release_zenodo_common_loaded", mode = "logical")) {
+  # Same self-locating guard-source idiom as `-package.R`'s docs/verify
+  # blocks (resolves this file's own directory from the active source()
+  # frame so the sibling common file loads regardless of cwd or how this
+  # file was sourced).
+  .analysis_release_zenodo_common_self_dir <- local({
+    ofiles <- Filter(Negate(is.null), lapply(sys.frames(), function(f) f$ofile))
+    if (length(ofiles) > 0) dirname(normalizePath(ofiles[[length(ofiles)]])) else NULL
+  })
+  .analysis_release_zenodo_common_candidates <- c(
+    if (!is.null(.analysis_release_zenodo_common_self_dir)) {
+      file.path(.analysis_release_zenodo_common_self_dir, "analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-common.R")
+    },
+    "functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-common.R",
+    "/app/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-common.R"
+  )
+  for (.analysis_release_zenodo_common_path in .analysis_release_zenodo_common_candidates) {
+    if (file.exists(.analysis_release_zenodo_common_path)) {
+      # local = TRUE: evaluate into THIS call's parent frame (same reasoning
+      # as `-package.R`'s guard blocks).
+      source(.analysis_release_zenodo_common_path, local = TRUE)
+      break
+    }
+  }
+  rm(
+    .analysis_release_zenodo_common_self_dir, .analysis_release_zenodo_common_candidates,
+    .analysis_release_zenodo_common_path
+  )
+}
+
 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
 # Shared constants (forbidden files/dirs, sensitive text, expected layout,
-# allowed file-type suffixes, the staging-ownership sentinel, the release-id
-# shape).
+# allowed file-type suffixes, the staging-ownership sentinel). The
+# release-id shape constant lives in the guard-sourced common.R above.
 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

 .ANALYSIS_RELEASE_ZENODO_FORBIDDEN_FILENAMES <- c(".env", ".env.local", ".envrc")
 .ANALYSIS_RELEASE_ZENODO_FORBIDDEN_DIR_PARTS <- c(
   ".git", ".planning", ".superpowers", ".venv", "__pycache__"
 )
 .ANALYSIS_RELEASE_ZENODO_SENSITIVE_TEXT_PATTERNS <- c(
   "/home/", "/users/", "bernt-popp", "zenodo_token", "zenodo_access_token",
   "bearer ", "development/sysndd", "development/nddscore", ".env", "git_sha"
 )
 # A well-formed analysis-release staging tree only ever contains these
 # suffixes (the release payload/docs). Doubles as both the file-type
 # ALLOWLIST (item 3: any staged regular file whose suffix is not in this set
 # fails validation, catching `.csv`/`.pem`/extensionless/binary secret files
 # without needing to scan binaries) and the set of suffixes the sensitive-
 # text content scan reads.
 .ANALYSIS_RELEASE_ZENODO_ALLOWED_SUFFIXES <- c(".md", ".json", ".sql", ".cff", ".sha256", ".txt")
 .ANALYSIS_RELEASE_ZENODO_EXPECTED_TOP_LEVEL <- c(
   "README.md", "DATA_CARD.md", "SCHEMA.md", "CHANGELOG.md", "CITATION.cff",
   "datapackage.json", "zenodo_metadata.json", "checksums.sha256"
 )
 .ANALYSIS_RELEASE_ZENODO_NESTED_DIR <- "analysis_snapshot_release"
 .ANALYSIS_RELEASE_ZENODO_NESTED_EXPECTED <- c("manifest.json", "checksums.sha256")

 # The ownership marker this tool writes at the staging root immediately
 # after creating it. `analysis_release_zenodo_package()` refuses to
 # recursively delete a pre-existing `--staging-dir` unless it is empty or
 # carries this sentinel -- see `.analysis_release_zenodo_staging_owned_by_tool()`.
 .ANALYSIS_RELEASE_ZENODO_STAGING_SENTINEL <- ".analysis-release-zenodo-staging"

-# A published release id is `"asr_" + 16 lowercase hex chars`

-# the ONLY shape ever allowed to become a filename, path component, or a
-# value written into the `latest.env` marker the Makefile `source`s.
-.ANALYSIS_RELEASE_ZENODO_ID_PATTERN <- "^asr_[0-9a-f]{16}$"

 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
 # Release-id validation -- path/filename/marker-injection guard (item 2).

+# shared `analysis-snapshot-release-zenodo-common.R` (guard-sourced above)
+# so the upload/DOI path validates identically -- see that file's header for
+# why (Codex round-2 finding: the upload path built an admin PATCH URL and a
+# printed shell command from an unvalidated `--release-id`).
 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

-#' Stop unless `release_id` is exactly `"latest"` (only when
-#' `allow_latest = TRUE`, i.e. the caller-supplied REQUEST arg) or a
-#' well-formed `asr_<16 lowercase hex>` id. Called on BOTH (a) the
-#' `--release-id` request argument (`allow_latest = TRUE`, in
-#' `analysis_release_zenodo_fetch_head()`) and (b) the RESOLVED id the API
-#' returns on the release head (`allow_latest = FALSE`, in
-#' `analysis_release_zenodo_package()`) -- defense-in-depth, since the
-#' resolved id is what actually becomes `<release_id>.tar.gz` and the
-#' `RELEASE_ID=` marker line. Any other value (`../evil`, an id containing a
-#' newline or shell metacharacters, ...) stops loudly here, before it is ever
-#' used as a path/filename/marker value.
-#'
-#' @return `release_id` (as a length-1 character), invisibly, on success.
-.analysis_release_zenodo_assert_valid_release_id <- function(release_id, allow_latest = FALSE) {
